steps.title: Clarify the named division
steps.content: The first sentence says biogeography is divided between dispersalists and vicariancists. The next sentence names those two camps and explains what each side believes: one side argues that dispersal determines species distribution, while the other insists that vicariance does. The transition must show that the second sentence clarifies or restates the division just introduced.

steps.title: Reject addition or contrast
steps.content: Choice A, Furthermore, would signal a separate added point, but the second sentence is explaining the same debate. Choice B, By contrast, would signal opposition between the two sentences, though they work together. Choice C, Similarly, would signal resemblance to a prior parallel point, which is not the relationship here. That is, correctly introduces a clarification of what the division means. The answer is D.
